decks.notes: The deck focuses on using Mishra's Workshop to accelerate the deployment of powerful artifacts like Golos, Tireless Pilgrim and Karn, the Great Creator. The deck also disrupts opponent's game plan with cards like Sphere of Resistance, Trinisphere, and Chalice of the Void. It also utilizes a combination of mana denial tactics with cards like Wasteland, Strip Mine, and Ghost Quarter. The deck aims to overwhelm opponents with aggressive artifact creatures like Stonecoil Serpent and make it difficult for the opponent to play spells efficiently through tax effects like Nettlecyst and Thorn of Amethyst.

This deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a fast and aggressive strategy, utilizing artifact creatures and powerful artifacts to disrupt the opponent's game plan. By using efficient mana sources like Mishra's Workshop and Tolarian Academy, as well as powerful artifacts like The One Ring and Mystic Forge, the deck can quickly deploy threats and lock down the opponent's options with cards like Chalice of the Void and Trinisphere. Additionally, cards like Tezzeret, Cruel Captain and Karn, the Great Creator provide additional utility and disruption, making it difficult for the opponent to recover once the deck gains control of the game.

The deck focuses on assembling the Coveted Jewel combo using cards like Tinker, Phyrexian Metamorph, and The One Ring to accelerate the game and control the board using artifacts like Mishra's Workshop and Grim Monolith. The deck also includes powerful spells like Force of Will and Ancestral Recall for disruption and card advantage. With the ability to generate a lot of mana early on and draw through the deck quickly, the deck aims to secure victory through overwhelming card advantage and board control.

A Vintage 1 vs 1 deck utilizing an Aggro - Hatebear archetype that aims to disrupt opponents' strategies with cards like Archon of Emeria and Chancellor of the Annex, while also applying pressure with cards like Clarion Conqueror and Seasoned Dungeoneer. The deck includes stax pieces like Void Mirror and Wasteland to further hinder opponents, while also utilizing powerful mana accelerants like Ancient Tomb and Black Lotus. The deck also includes creature removal options like Swords to Plowshares and protection tools like Cavern of Souls and Karakas to ensure its creatures can stick around to close out the game.

The deck focuses on disrupting opponents with cards like Force of Will, Collector Ouphe, and Abrupt Decay while using powerful creatures like Leovold, Emissary of Trest and Murktide Regent to apply pressure. The deck also utilizes cards like Ancestral Recall, Brainstorm, and Gush to draw cards and maintain card advantage. Additionally, the deck features cards like Deathrite Shaman and Treasure Cruise to help control the board and outvalue opponents. The mana base is also streamlined with fetch lands and dual lands to ensure consistent access to the deck's powerful spells.

The main concept of this Combo - Dredge deck is to quickly fill up the graveyard with cards like Golgari Thug, Stinkweed Imp, and Golgari Grave-Troll, in order to abuse graveyard mechanics such as Dredge and trigger effects like Narcomoeba and Prized Amalgam. The deck aims to overwhelm opponents with a flood of creatures that can return from the graveyard, allowing for explosive plays and a quick victory. The inclusion of cards like Force of Will and Cabal Therapy provide interaction and disruption against opponents while maintaining the deck's primary objective of comboing off.

This deck focuses on quickly disrupting the opponent's strategy by using artifact-based Aggro creatures and mana denial cards. The deck aims to lock down the opponent's resources through cards like Trinisphere, Nettlecyst, and Null Rod, while also applying pressure with efficient threats like Golos, Tireless Pilgrim and Scrawling Crawler. With access to powerful mana ramp with cards like Mishra's Workshop and Ancient Tomb, the deck can deploy its threats quickly and overwhelm the opponent before they can stabilize. Additionally, Karn, the Great Creator and Mystic Forge provide additional utility and card advantage.

The deck focuses on utilizing hatebears and aggressive creatures to disrupt the opponent's game plan while applying pressure on the board. By combining cards that limit the opponent's resources with efficient creatures like Anointed Peacekeeper and Archon of Emeria, the deck aims to control the game and win through creature damage. The inclusion of disruptive artifacts like Chancellor of the Annex and Void Mirror further enhances the deck's ability to hinder the opponent's strategy. With a mix of disruption and aggression, this deck aims to overwhelm the opponent and secure victory through creature combat.

The deck focuses on utilizing fast mana sources like Mishra's Workshop, Black Lotus, and mana rocks to quickly ramp into powerful artifacts like Coveted Jewel and The One Ring. The goal is to generate a large amount of mana and draw cards with Paradoxical Outcome, eventually using Tezzeret and other control elements to protect the combo and lock out the opponent. The deck aims to win through massive card advantage and overwhelming the opponent with efficient artifact-based spells.

This Vintage Control deck focuses on utilizing Oath of Druids to cheat powerful creatures like Atraxa, Grand Unifier into play early. The deck also includes a mix of disruption spells like Force of Will and Pyroblast, along with card draw and selection spells to help find key pieces. The mana base includes dual lands and fetch lands for consistency, along with utility lands like Boseiju, Who Endures and Strip Mine. Overall, the deck aims to control the game early and then overwhelm the opponent with powerful threats.

The concept of this deck is to quickly fill your graveyard with cards like Serum Powder, Narcomoeba, Ichorid, and Golgari Thug, and then use cards like Creeping Chill, Prized Amalgam, and Hollow One to generate value from your graveyard. The deck also includes disruptive cards like Force of Will and Force of Negation to protect your combo pieces. Bazaar of Baghdad is essential for filling your graveyard quickly, while Shambling Shell and Stinkweed Imp help dredge through your deck. The goal is to overwhelm your opponent with a steady stream of threats and graveyard recursion.

The main concept of this Dimir Control deck is to disrupt the opponent's game plan using a combination of counter spells, removal, and hand disruption while maintaining card advantage through powerful draw spells. The deck aims to control the game tempo, protect its threats, and outvalue the opponent with efficient cards like Force of Will, Fatal Push, and Lórien Revealed. Utilizing cards like Ancestral Recall, Dig Through Time, and Treasure Cruise, the deck can consistently find its key pieces and answer threats efficiently. Overall, the deck focuses on resource management and disrupting the opponent's strategy to secure victory.

This deck focuses on utilizing powerful combo pieces like Paradoxical Outcome, Underworld Breach, and Tolarian Academy to establish a strong board presence and generate massive card advantage. By leveraging the synergy between artifacts and spells, the deck aims to create explosive turns that overwhelm the opponent and secure a quick victory. The inclusion of cards like Force of Will, Mental Misstep, and Flusterstorm helps protect the combo pieces and disrupt the opponent's strategy. Additionally, the presence of Lurrus of the Dream-Den as a companion provides an alternate game plan and additional resilience to removal spells.

The deck focuses on using various control elements to disrupt the opponent's game plan while assembling its own resources and winning conditions. It includes a mix of permission spells, removal, card draw, and powerful artifacts like Black Lotus to generate mana advantage. Additionally, it utilizes a companion in Lurrus of the Dream-Den to provide additional value and resilience. The deck aims to outvalue and outmaneuver the opponent through efficient use of its cards and strategic decision-making, ultimately controlling the game and emerging victorious.

This deck aims to utilize the card Paradoxical Outcome to generate significant card advantage and mana acceleration. By bouncing multiple nonland permanents back to their hand, the player can draw additional cards and then replay those cards for added value. The combination of efficient mana rocks like Moxen and powerful draw spells like Ancestral Recall and Time Walk allows the player to quickly assemble a winning board state or combo off to win the game. The deck also includes disruptive elements like Force of Will and Swords to Plowshares to interact with the opponent's game plan.

This deck aims to quickly assemble powerful mana and draw engines, utilizing artifact synergies to generate massive advantage. The key goal is to use cards like Tinker and Paradoxical Outcome to find and recur Coveted Jewel, while disrupting opponents' strategies with cards like Force of Will. Once Coveted Jewel is on the battlefield, the deck can generate huge amounts of mana and card advantage to overwhelm opponents, while also using Time Vault and other artifacts to control the game and secure victory.
